title: Father's 'Govardhan Parikrama' in Mathura pays off as Mayank makes fiery return
content: Two years after injuries threatened to derail his career, Mayank Yadav announced his return with a fiery 2 for 18 against Zimbabwe. While his father prayed for his fitness during the Govardhan Parikrama in Mathura, the Delhi pacer touched 149 kph and reminded everyone why the Indian cricket board never stopped believing in him.

title: 'Don't insult students' intelligence': Rahul slams PM Modi's midnight video on paper leaks
content: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi criticised Prime Minister Modi's video message regarding the NEET-UG paper leak. He demanded the education minister's resignation and punishment for those involved. Prime Minister Modi announced a new bill with stringent punishments for exam paper leaks. This proposed legislation will be discussed in the Union Cabinet soon.

title: US new visa restriction targets individuals involved in cybercrime
content: The United States has introduced a new global visa restriction policy. This measure targets individuals involved in cybercrime and cyber-enabled offenses. Online investment scams and sextortion perpetrators face these new restrictions. Immediate family members of those responsible may also be subjected to visa limitations. The administration is deploying various tools to dismantle criminal scam networks.

title: Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike; PM Modi vows stricter actions against paper leaks
content: Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after government assurances on student demands. Prime Minister Modi announced a new bill to combat examination paper leaks. This proposed legislation includes fast-track courts and tougher punishments for offenders. The Union Cabinet will discuss the bill on Friday before Parliament next week. The government aims to protect students' interests and make the examination system foolproof.

title: 'My last match was World Cup final': Argentina star announces retirement
content: Argentina defender Nicolas Otamendi has retired from international football after a 17-year career. The 38-year-old made 139 appearances, winning the 2022 FIFA World Cup and two Copa America titles. His final match was Argentina's 1-0 World Cup final defeat to Spain. Otamendi thanked Argentina in an emotional farewell, saying he leaves "with my head held high".

title: ‘Paper leak’ at Uttarakhand Technical University, 2 BTech exams cancelled
content: Veer Madho Singh Bhandari Uttarakhand Technical University cancelled two BTech exams after a paper leak allegation. An assistant professor was arrested and his services terminated following the university's complaint. Protests erupted on campus as students demanded accountability from the administration. The university cited upholding credibility and student interest for the cancellations. This incident adds to Uttarakhand's history of recurring examination paper scandals.

title: Canada college to protesting Indian students: Seek legal advice, retain imp documents
content: Indian students protest after Portage College's business management diploma graduates faced work visa rejections.  The Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada stated their programs were not eligible for permits.  Portage College encourages affected students to retain important documents and seek legal advice.  The college cannot overturn decisions made by the immigration authority.  Affected students should consult official government materials and obtain independent advice.

title: Florida’s python hunt used 3M camera photos and found only four snakes
content: In Florida, scientists discovered that captive female pythons failed to attract wild males. Utilizing artificial intelligence, researchers sifted through millions of camera images, revealing few python sightings. Additionally, attempts to use mammalian scent lures for detecting the invasive Burmese pythons proved futile. However, there is optimism as removal strategies in the Everglades are progressing, bolstered by hunting and radio tracking initiatives aimed at controlling the invasive species population.

title: Ships trapped near Strait of Hormuz could become nurseries for invasive species
content: Ships lingering in the Persian Gulf could unintentionally facilitate the spread of harmful marine invaders worldwide. Extended anchorage allows marine organisms to thrive on the hulls, creating significant biofouling concerns that threaten global maritime commerce. These invasive species can inflict extensive ecological and economic harm on coastal areas. It is imperative for nations to join forces to address biofouling issues and prevent future outbreaks of invasion.

title: Jeff Bezos' 'not-so-happy' meeting may bring big changes for Prime Video subscribers
content: Jeff Bezos is pushing Prime Video executives to integrate artificial intelligence prominently.  This project, known as Lighthouse, aims to showcase Amazon's AI investments globally.  The streaming service's home screen is being redesigned with AI-driven recommendations.  This strategic shift could impact traditional revenue streams for media companies.  Deeper integration with the Alexa assistant is also being considered.

title: Jos Buttler scripts history, surpasses Chris Gayle on all-time T20 run charts
content: Jos Buttler has climbed to second on the all-time T20 run charts. He surpassed Chris Gayle during a recent match at Lord's.  Buttler now trails Kieron Pollard by a narrow margin for the top spot. This milestone follows a period of strong batting form for England's star. He has rediscovered his touch in recent tournaments and matches.

title: DA urges silence as Nolan Wells death investigation continues
content: Mississippi District Attorney Angel Myers McIlrath has urged investigators to avoid public statements as the Nolan Wells death investigation heads to a grand jury, saying silence is necessary to protect the integrity of the case. Her remarks follow inconclusive independent autopsy findings that left several questions unanswered. Officials continue reviewing evidence, while toxicology results remain pending. No arrests or charges have been announced, and authorities say the investigation remains active.

title: How Serena Williams turned childhood poverty into tennis greatness
content: Serena Williams grew up in a family that struggled financially but never gave up on their dream. Guided by her parents, Richard Williams and Oracene Price, she trained on public courts with limited resources before becoming one of tennis' greatest champions. Her journey from humble beginnings to 23 Grand Slam singles titles remains an inspiring story of resilience, sacrifice and unwavering family support.

title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce's newlywed bliss tested by viral relationship claims
content: A resurfaced social media post has reignited debate over Taylor Swift's past relationship with Joe Alwyn, claiming she admitted to cheating with Matty Healy through her song lyrics. The claim remains unverified and reflects fan speculation rather than fact. Meanwhile, attention has also shifted to Swift's reported July 3 wedding to Travis Kelce at Madison Square Garden, as fans continue discussing both her music and personal life.

title: UP polls: 'UP ke ladke' together again to take on BJP, but who will show 'bigger heart'?
content: Congress and Samajwadi Party leaders seek a 'big heart' from each other for upcoming UP elections.  Rahul Gandhi assured unity, while UP Congress leaders demand equal status.  Both parties cite past election results to justify their seat claims.  Past alliance failures in Bihar and Haryana highlight potential friction points.  Successful seat-sharing is crucial for the INDIA bloc to challenge the BJP.

title: 'Jana Nayagan' BO day 1: Thalapathy Vijay's farewell film opens strong
content: Thalapathy Vijay's latest blockbuster, 'Jana Nayagan,' kicked off impressively at the box office, raking in an astounding forty-one crore rupees net in India and a whopping seventy-eight crore globally. The film, directed by H. Vinoth, showcases Vijay in an action-packed role as a former police officer, with Tamil Nadu delivering the highest state collections, signifying widespread audience enthusiasm.

title: Chess legend Anand steps in as FIDE interim president after EU sanctions sideline Dvorkovich
content: FIDE President Arkady Dvorkovich has voluntarily stepped aside after being sanctioned by the European Union over Russia's invasion of Ukraine. Calling the sanctions "unlawful and unfair", he said he would challenge them legally. Deputy President Viswanathan Anand will serve as interim president. Meanwhile, India named its squads for the FIDE Chess Olympiad, with D Gukesh and Koneru Humpy leading the teams.

title: Oil holds above $100 as Red Sea attacks and Kazakhstan outage tighten supplies
content: Oil prices have sharply rebounded, exceeding one hundred dollars per barrel. Attacks on Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea have revived supply fears. Kazakhstan's oil exports also faced disruptions, adding to global concerns. This marks a significant reversal from recent price declines seen earlier. These events have tightened the noose around global energy supply routes.

title: 'Where is Patidar?' - Ex-India batter questions selectors over Zimbabwe T20I squad
content: Hanuma Vihari questioned India's decision to leave Rajat Patidar out of the Zimbabwe T20I squad despite his strong IPL 2026 season and leadership with RCB. He also criticised the omissions of Kuldeep Yadav and Sanju Samson, saying the squad lacked right-handed batters. Meanwhile, India beat Zimbabwe by seven wickets in the first T20I to take a 1-0 series lead.

title: Captaincy weighed him down, freedom set him free: The remarkable second act of Root
content: Joe Root's remarkable consistency guides England to ODI series victory. He scored 249 runs across three innings, proving his enduring value. Root's game has evolved, making him a headache for opposing teams. His enduring passion fuels his desire to be England's greatest batter. Root's presence provides comfort and freedom for younger English players.

title: 'Not up to the standard': Zimbabwe captain Raza blames pitch for India defeat
content: Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza criticised the pitch after his side's seven-wicket loss to India in the first T20I, saying early moisture made batting difficult and the surface became easier later. He hoped for fairer conditions in the next match. Raza also praised Wessly Madhevere and Tadiwanashe Marumani for helping Zimbabwe recover from 32/4 to post a competitive total.

title: 2,500 detained, homes of 2 LeT terrorists razed in Kashmir after cop killing
content: Security forces demolished two Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorist homes overnight in Kashmir. Over two thousand five hundred people were detained for questioning following a police killing. The operation aimed to dismantle terrorist support networks across the region. Officials reviewed security arrangements for the ongoing Amarnath Yatra pilgrimage. Political leaders criticized the demolitions and mass detentions as counter-productive.

title: Pvt investment in space sector crosses $618 million: Minister Jitendra Singh to RS
content: India's space sector has seen unprecedented momentum and growth. Private investment has now crossed six hundred million dollars. Over four hundred active space startups are contributing to national ambitions. Indian private companies have successfully demonstrated launch and orbital capabilities. Robust national security safeguards are being strengthened alongside commercial expansion.

title: NIA arrests absconding PFI district chief in Kerala's Sreenivasan murder case
content: The National Investigation Agency arrested a key absconder in the Sreenivasan murder case. Muhammadali K.P, a PFI Palakkad district president, was apprehended upon arriving from Saudi Arabia. He was a prominent perpetrator and harbored assailants involved in the brutal killing of Sreenivasan. The PFI conspiracy aimed to establish Islamic Rule in India by 2047. Investigations revealed PFI imparted arms training and collected target information through its various wings.

title: 'In view of possible violence': Why Sonam Wangchuk ended hunger strike after 26 days
content: Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after prolonged negotiations and to prevent potential violence. Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced tougher measures against examination paper leaks. The Union Cabinet will consider stricter punishments and fast-track courts for offenders. The government aims to introduce legislation during the ongoing Monsoon Session of Parliament. Wangchuk urged everyone to remain vigilant against any form of violence.

title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce reportedly have bigger plans for wedding photos
content: Taylor Swift has reportedly delayed releasing her wedding photos with Travis Kelce because she is waiting for the "perfect moment." Sources claim the singer wants the images to be part of a larger storytelling project, possibly a documentary or coffee-table book, rather than a magazine exclusive. Insiders say Swift prefers to control her narrative, creating meaningful cultural moments instead of chasing publicity or financial gain.

title: 'Talk without condition such as resignation': Govt to oppn leaders over NEET debate
content: The government is engaging opposition leaders to resolve the Parliament deadlock over the NEET paper leak.  Discussions aim to end disruptions which have stalled proceedings since the Monsoon session began.  The Centre is ready for debate without preconditions, including minister's resignation.  A meeting of floor leaders is likely soon to build consensus for the discussion.  This outreach follows repeated adjournments due to protests on examination irregularities.

title: 18-storey building of an apartment complex in south Bengaluru to be demolished
content: According to GBA sources, the tower slated for demolition is one of the five wings of a residential complex

title: Western Ganga theme chosen for Lalbagh’s Independence Day flower show
content: Horticulture Department had sent around 20 options to the officials concerned and the Chief Minister’s Office

title: How should cities reclaim footpaths? | Explained
content: Why was the Street Vendors Act enacted? What does the Act require before vendors can be removed? What is the role of the Town Vending Committee? Can street vendors be evicted before a survey is completed? Why has Bengaluru’s footpath drive come under scrutiny?

title: U.S. slaps 10% tariffs on goods imported from India over issue of forced labour
content: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer announced the new tariffs on 60 countries under Section 301 of the Trade Act on July 24, a day before the expiration of 10% additional levies on all countries

title: CJP protests | From exam paper leaks to unemployment, a generation at breaking point
content: For many young Indians, a loss of trust in education and rising economic insecurity are giving way to anger, anxiety and collective action

title: Tech Mahindra employee protests outside campus over alleged termination after availing leave to care for ailing daughter
content: In response, Tech Mahindra said it had connected with the employee and was reviewing the matter with urgency.

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Govt. has agreed to our request to hold talks today at neutral venue, says CJP
content: CJP's peaceful protest at Jantar Mantar will continue until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns, says CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke

title: Over 6.17 lakh Waqf properties approved on UMEED portal
content: The portal is part of government’s ongoing initiative to digitise and streamline Waqf property records

title: Bengaluru: Criminal cases booked against 249 guest lecturers who submitted fake PhD certificates
content: Even as the recruitment process for guest lecturers in government first grade colleges colleges for the year 2026-27 is under way, tainted candidates have been blacklisted and permanently excluded from the process.

title: IndiGo, Air India oppose Adani’s airline entry
content: It is a recipe for conflict of interest as well as squeezing other players; such a consolidation across the value chain not only kills competition but also results in fewer jobs, said an Air India official

title: No debate without Pradhan’s resignation, Opposition tells govt. as it hits the streets
content: INDIA bloc closes ranks on paper leaks, rejects discussion offer and carries protest from Parliament to Gandhi Smriti in a show of solidarity with agitating students; in the meeting, it was felt that a debate would offer the govt. a chance to grandstand its way through by leaning on its numerical strength in both Houses of Parliament

title: Turmoil in Tinsel Town: Malayalam movie actors’ fights turn from reel to real
content: The internal bickering in the Association of Malayalam Movie Artistes, the actors’ organisation, has hit a new low with public spats, court cases and police complaints. G. Krishnakumar tracks the developments in the association

title: PM reaches out to students; Sonam Wangchuk ends fast
content: Activist ends his hunger strike in the presence of two Ministers; Modi, in a late-night social media post, says Cabinet will discuss paper leak issue today; Centre transfers Higher Education Secretary

title: Odisha proposes radical reform in policy “Nutan Sakala” to curb trafficking of women, girls
content: Trafficking profile of the State raises concern as it has been recording highest number of human trafficking victims annually in India since 2021 with “near-zero” conviction rates in certain years

title: Sikkim green group announces “save Teesta” foot march
content: The Affected Citizens of Teesta group seeks a fresh Environmental Impact Assessment and Social Impact Assessment for all hydropower projects, including NHPC’s Stage-VI, where 22 workers died in a tunnel on July 20

title: Manipur SIR: Search and download the list of deleted voters
content: Look up any of the 1,58,677 electors deleted from Manipur's 2026 draft SIR electoral rolls. Pick an Assembly constituency, then search by name, EPIC number, related person's name, or booth.

title: Burdened by debt, bonded to life of toil
content: Unable to repay debts, a few members of the Vadde community in Andhra Pradesh’s Anantapur district were forced to work as bonded labourers at an illegal stone-cutting unit for more than a year. Haresh P. finds out how they were rescued by the authorities from the adverse conditions

title: Govt. agreed to our request to hold talks on July 24 at neutral venue, says CJP
content: “A discussion has been called for, we are very happy that the government has agreed to our request that it be at a neutral venue. The Constitution Club of India has been chosen,” Mr. Das said.

title: Telangana farmers caught between a dry spell and despair
content: Delayed rains, falling groundwater levels and the looming impact of El Niño have left thousands of farmers across Telangana anxiously watching the skies. While the State has rolled out a contingency plan urging a shift to less water-intensive crops, many cultivators have stayed with their traditional choices, hoping the monsoon will revive. As reservoirs run low and crop stress mounts, they remain caught between official advisories and age-old cropping patterns, report K. Shiva Shanker and P. Laxma Reddy

title: Pre-poll promises, post-poll precarity
content: Launched in 2024 ahead of the Maharashtra Assembly election, the Ladki Bahin scheme initially delivered a vital financial lifeline to farm widows in the mostly rain-starved Marathwada region. But with the political dust settled, mass deletions of beneficiaries have abruptly turned that brief security into deep anxiety, finds Radheshyam Jadhav

title: Jantar Mantar protest updates: Wangchuk breaks 26-day fast; relieved, says CJP founder Dipke, but vows to continue protest till Pradhan resigns
content: He broke the fast in the presence of two Union Ministers on certain conditions, he says; the government gave a written assurance that cases against students will be taken back if they are not violent, Mr. Dorjay says; the government also gave a written assurance that they will consider compensation for NEET aspirants who took their lives, he adds

title: Utilisation Certificates of ₹92, 132.75 crore yet to be received by office, says CAG report, 2024-25 in Bihar
content: “62,632 outstanding UCs of ₹ 92,132.75 crore were yet to be received by the Principal Accountant General (Accounts & Entitlements), Bihar as on March 31, 2025”, said the CAG report under the title of “Financial Reporting Practices”, which was laid on the floor of the state assembly on July 23

title: U.P. oldest MLA Alam Badi Azmi dies, leaders express grief
content: He represented the Nizamabad constituency five times between 1996 and 2007 and again from 2012 until now. Leaders from across parties expressed grief over the death

title: Coast Guard arrests four arms smugglers off Sundarbans
content: The operation led to the recovery of a cache of illegal weapons and was executed in close coordination with the Sunderbans Forest Department and intelligence agencies

title: Activist on hunger strike over Ken-Betwa river link project ends fast after 18 days
content: Mr. Bhatnagar of Jai Kisan Sangathan had been on a hunger strike for 18 days and was leading the protest, where several people from Chhatarpur and Panna districts had been demonstrating since July 4 against the ₹44,000-crore project of the Union Government by tying nooses around their necks and lying on makeshift pyres in the river in a symbolic protest

title: International waterways must remain safe and unimpeded: S. Jaishankar
content: Attacks on seafarers, civilian shipping or infrastructure cannot be countenanced, says External Affairs Minister at East Asia Summit; Ministry hits back at Pakistan Deputy PM’s comments on Kashmir and Indus Water Treaty

title: Flood toll climbs to 47 in Assam; over 7 lakh affected
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says localised heavy rainfall 436% above normal and cloudbursts upstream in Nagaland combined to cause the unprecedented devastation

title: BJP leaders, allies urge Centre to treat youth protests with empathy
content: Former HRD Minister Murli Manohar Joshi says the protest should not be treated as a law-and-order problem; sections of the BJP and its allies in the NDA feel the issue has gone beyond NEET and education

title: Sonam Wangchuk has broken his fast, says Leh Apex Body co-convenor
content: He broke the fast in the presence of two Union Ministers on certain conditions, he says;  Govt gave a written assurance that cases against students will be taken back, if they are not violent, Mr. Dorjay says

title: Protests over paper leak intensify in States
content: Student outfits, civil society groups, and political parties hit the streets demanding resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan; protest marches turn confrontational in several districts of Kerala; police stop protesters in A.P.; security personnel resort to baton-charge in Bihar

title: Three days since July 20 violence, Delhi Police, MHA yet to acknowledge police excesses
content: Eenesh, a resident of Chhattisgarh, who took part in the protest, refuted the police claim that protesters threw bricks at them

title: CPI criticises Kerala Chief Minister on Waqf issue
content: The reconstitution of the Board by including two non-Muslims had grown into a conflict issue between the Kerala Waqf Board and the State government

title: Governor emphasises need for coordination of tradition and technology
content: Governor of Karnataka Thawar Chand Gehlot has emphasised the need for coordinating heritage with modern technology so as to introduce and popularise Ayurveda at the global level

title: PM Modi promises 'more strict actions against paper leaks' amid CJP protests
content: In a self-recorded video shared on X, the Prime Minister announced that the Union Cabinet will take up a draft Bill providing for Fast-Track Courts and stringent punishment for offenders. he said, “I have issued directions to departments to set up Fast-Track Courts

title: Hundreds gather in Kerala’s Kochi to express solidarity with Delhi student protest
content: Participants express solidarity with the ongoing student protests in New Delhi over the alleged NEET question paper leak and condemn police action against the protesters

title: Aadhaar verification for Tatkal bookings not violative of privacy rights: Kerala High Court
content: Court notes that the requirement for authentication for Tatkal tickets was justified based on experiences of alleged deceit, manipulation and bulk booking, which denied access to the ticketing system for genuine users

title: Vijay’s Jana Nayagan hits the screens across State; cinemas see good footfall
content: Cinema owners in the southern districts report a noticeable increase in women turning up for ‘first day’ shows. For Friday, most cinemas in Chennai say occupancy levels are around 70%-75%

title: World Hepatitis Day webinar to be held on July 27
content: Experts will discuss causes, transmission, prevention, early detection and advances in treatment, while addressing common misconceptions about viral hepatitis

title: Protests demanding NEET ban gather momentum across city
content: Filmmaker Pa. Ranjith-led Neelam Students Movement staging a protest at Rajarathinam Stadium on Thursday, demanding educational reforms and the abolition of NEET.

title: A namesake creates trouble for former Kerala Minister P.K. Sreemathi
content: CPI(M) leader accused of drawing a pension claiming to be the wife of a former MLA. Allegation stems from confusion with another P.K. Sreemathi, widow of former Kunnamkulam MLA P.R. Krishnan, who received the pension till 2020

title: Bengaluru rallies behind Delhi student protest, demand resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan
content: Raising slogans, the protestors lit candles and switched on their mobile torches as night descended, in an expression of solidarity. They held placards with clever puns and word play

title: Farmers oppose acquisition of land for HAL expansion at Gubbi in Tumakuru district
content: Farmers are worried that HAL’s long-term plans to expand the facility by adding infrastructure such as a hospital and other amenities for its employees could lead to further acquisition of agricultural land

title: Set KPSC in order before protesting over NEET, BJP tells Karnataka Congress
content: The Opposition BJP alleges that the government here has failed to fix responsibility for the death of 11 youth in the stampede near Chinnaswamy Stadium and for the lathi-charge on unemployed youth in North Karnataka who were protesting the delay in recruitment for government jobs

title: Indian Migrant Worker Found Dead At Jerusalem Flat, Israeli Man Arrested
content: The investigation indicates a criminal background, and a 31-year-old man from the southern city of Eilat has been arrested on suspicion of involvement in the murder.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Markets Likely To Fall As Brent Crude Tops $100 Amid US-Iran War
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices back at $100: Brent crude hits 2-month high as Red Sea attacks stoke supply fears.

title: "In View Of Possible Violence": Sonam Wangchuk Ends Hunger Strike On 26th Day
content: Sonam Wangchuk said his decision to end his fast followed a long negotiation on various conditions and in view of possible violence in the country.

title: 'Assured Paper Leak Discussion In Parliament': Sonam Wangchuk On Ending Fast
content: Sonam Wangchuk, who had been on a hunger strike since June 28, broke his fast at Gurugram's Medanta Hospital on the government's assurance that no legal action would be taken against the Jantar Mantar protesters.

title: Meet Naresh Pal Gangwar, Picked As New Education Secretary Amid CJP Protest
content: Born on October 21, 1970, in Nainital, Gangwar completed a B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering, followed by an M.Tech in Communication and Radar. He also holds a Master's degree in Economics.

title: Before You Turn 40, Make Sure You've Hit These 5 Money Milestones
content: Higher salaries encourage lifestyle upgrades.But credit card dues, personal loans and other high-interest borrowings can erode wealth over time.

title: Trump Hits China, Israel With 12.5% New Tariffs. Why He Put India At 10%
content: Nations that enforced such laws -- including India, Sri Lanka, and the European Union -- will be hit with 10 per cent levies if they fail to enforce them.

title: CJP Protest LIVE Updates: CJP, Union Ministers May Meet At 12.30 PM Today, Say Sources
content: CJP Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters are continuing their sit-in at Jantar Mantar.

title: "Follow Medical Advice": PM's Message To Sonam Wangchuk After Ending Fast
content: Sonam Wangchuk, who had been on a hunger strike since June 28, broke his fast at Gurugram's Medanta Hospital on the government's assurance that no legal action would be taken against the Jantar Mantar protesters.

title: 'Correcting Human Rights Abuse': US Slaps New Tariffs On India, 59 Countries
content: The United States said Thursday that it would impose new tariffs on 60 trading partners over forced labor concerns, replacing an expiring global duty rolled out by President Donald Trump earlier this year.

title: 22,000 Relocated, 1,200 Rescued As Flood-Like Situation Worsens In Gujarat
content: Gujarat Rains: Navsari and Valsad were the worst affected districts followed by Surat, Ahmedabad, Kheda Tapi and Dangs where persistent downpour since Wednesday threw normal life out of gear.

title: Trump Imposes Fresh 10% US Tariff On India In New Trade Move
content: The United States unveiled fresh tariff slabs of 10 and 12.5 per cent on several economies under its Section 301 on Thursday.

title: US Strikes Iran As Tensions Escalate Over Shipping Routes In Hormuz
content: The US military announced Thursday that it is conducting the 13th night of strikes against Iran as clashes escalate over shipping routes.

title: Government Bars Communication Infra Providers From Sharing Data Outside India
content: The government has barred communication infrastructure providers, including those involved in cloud-based networks for telecom services, mobile towers and satellite gateways, from sharing any data outside the country, according to an official notification.

title: US Says Won't Block Tate Brothers' Extradition To UK Over Rape Charges
content: US President Donald Trump will not stop the extradition of influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan to Britain on sex crime charges, the White House said on Thursday.

title: Trump Says Frozen Iranian Assets Will Pay For Damage To Shipping
content: President Donald Trump said Thursday that the US would begin using frozen Iranian assets to pay for ships and cargo damaged by strikes in and around the Gulf.

title: Rahul Gandhi's protest outside PM house ‘insincere’: Sonam Wangchuk’s wife
content: sonam Wangchuk's wife, Gitanjali J Angmo, criticizes the flash protest as "insincere" the protest led by Congress MP Rahul Gandhi near the PM's residence on July 21 . angmo: "this is an awakened India. they cannot gaslight or be insincere anymore"

title: SC lifts 2-year-old blanket ban on setting up of industries in Taj zone
content: SC lifted its two-year-old blanket ban on setting up industries in Taj Trapezium Zone . development comes a few months ahead of UP assembly polls . UP govt says industrial activities, especially when it involves establishment of non-polluting industrial units, cannot be brought to a grinding halt .

title: Fast-track courts, bill to tackle leaks: PM Modi
content: the union cabinet will discuss a bill to further strengthen the anti-cheating law . govt will seek its passage at the earliest . "fast-track courts and strict punishment will be discussed in the Cabinet tomorrow," modi says .

title: Won’t forget India’s rescue of 12 Chinese crew members : China's defence attache
content: china's newly appointed Defence Attache to India, Rear Admiral You Jian, thanked the Indian military for rescuing 12 Chinese crew members last year . you said last month that the rescue operation was aimed at improving relations between the two countries .

title: No Parliament business, four days into monsoon session
content: monsoon session of parliament staring at a washout . opposition blasted invitations from govt for a discussion . stalemate could not be broken despite Speaker Om Birla meeting Rahul Gandhi and Akhilesh Yadav .

title: India invites Bangladesh PM to Brics Summit
content: india invites Bangladesh PM DHAKA/NEW DELHI to attend the 18th Brics summit . the summit will focus on global cooperation, trade, and sustainable development . india will host the summit in new Delhi on september 12-13 .

title: CJP delegation to meet government ministers on Friday as Centre renews outreach: Report
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." title: MPs ask why govt didn’t inform owners of non-E20 vehicles about mileage loss
content: members of the parliamentary committee on transport asked top government officials on why no effort was made to inform owners of non-E20 compliant vehicles about potential mileage loss due to use of 20% ethanol blended petrol in these vehicles . some members also asked officials whether any economic analysis has been carried out to assess whether E20 fuel has had any gain to individual vehicle owners .

title: 2 new secretaries in education ministry, Vineet Joshi moved to panchayati raj
content: govt named new secretaries for higher and school education departments . incumbent Vineet Joshi moved to low-profile panchayati raj department . animal husbandry secretary TK Anil Kumar will be the new school education secretary .

title: SC laments vanishing of joint family system
content: SC expressed concern about the vanishing of the joint family system . it would lead to "disaster" as the new generation would be unaware of family bonding and relationships . the court said that it is unfortunate that people nowadays rely on WhatsApp-circulated messages .

title: Road crashes killed a record 1.8 lakh in 2025; 502 people daily
content: road crashes claimed a little over 1.8 lakh lives in 2025, translating into 502 deaths per day . the highest increase in such fatalities was reported in Uttar Pradesh . death tol lrises ministry says data for west Bengal has been taken from eDAR records .
